raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want A) strictly sentence-level copyediting only (grammar, clarity, concision; no restructuring or reframing), or B) copyediting plus light restructuring and argument tightening (possible reordering and modest reframing of claims)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polishing for clarity, concision, and tone without changing technical content.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve and, where needed, clarify expressions of uncertainty so readers understand the limits of the claims.",
    "RX.2": "Polishing must not inadvertently strengthen claims beyond what the described evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the requested cleanup scope and avoid introducing new methods, results, or citations.",
    "RX.4": "Language changes should not encourage risky deployment decisions or misinterpretation of performance or safety.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should retain original technical details and citations to maintain provenance and traceability of claims."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want A) strictly sentence-level copyediting only (grammar, clarity, concision; no restructuring or reframing), or B) copyediting plus light restructuring and argument tightening (possible reordering and modest reframing of claims)?

rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polishing for clarity, concision, and tone without changing technical content.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve and, where needed, clarify expressions of uncertainty so readers understand the limits of the claims.
rationale.RX.2: Polishing must not inadvertently strengthen claims beyond what the described evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the requested cleanup scope and avoid introducing new methods, results, or citations.
rationale.RX.4: Language changes should not encourage risky deployment decisions or misinterpretation of performance or safety.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should retain original technical details and citations to maintain provenance and traceability of claims.
